# agentic-kanban — Expanded Reference

> Agent coordination engine — shared task state for cooperating agents. One SQLite file. Zero infrastructure.

## Primitives

### Task states

```
TODO → IN_PROGRESS → IN_REVIEW → DONE
                   → BLOCKED
IN_REVIEW → TODO (rejected)
```

### Lease system

- Each claimed task gets a 15-minute lease
- `log-progress` renews the lease (heartbeat)
- `extend-lease` extends without logging
- Expired leases are reclaimed lazily on next `claim-next`
- Crash recovery: agent dies → lease expires → work becomes claimable

### Dependencies

- Tasks support `depends_on` (comma-separated task IDs)
- `claim-next --respect-deps` (default: true) skips tasks with unmet deps
- `plan lint` detects cycles and missing deps

### Events / Hooks

10 events fire on state transitions. Place executables in `.kanban/hooks/`:

- `task.created` — task dispatched
- `task.claimed` — agent claims a task
- `task.progress` — progress logged
- `task.completed` — task finished
- `task.submitted_for_review` — submitted for review
- `task.blocked` — blocked
- `review.approved` — approved
- `review.rejected` — rejected
- `task.priority_updated` — batch priority update
- `task.project_updated` — batch project update

Hooks receive JSON on stdin, 30s timeout, `.d/` dir for concurrency.

---

## CLI Commands

### Setup

```
kanban init [--harness generic] [--plan plan.md] [--dir]
  Scaffold database, agent files, and skill files

kanban re-init
  Re-scaffold agent files without touching the database
```

### Task Operations

```
kanban task dispatch --title --role [--project] [--priority] [--depends-on]
  Create a task. Priority lower=more urgent, default 100.

kanban task claim <id> --agent
  Claim a specific task by ID (hierarchical delegation)

kanban task claim-next --agent --role [--count N] [--project] [--respect-deps]
  Atomically claim highest-priority task. --count N for batch.

kanban task log-progress <id> --agent --note [--type]
  Log progress and renew 15-minute lease. Types: PROGRESS, ERROR, DECISION

kanban task extend-lease <id> --agent [--minutes]
  Extend lease without logging progress (heartbeat)

kanban task block <id> --agent --reason
  Mark blocked and clear lease

kanban task complete <id> --agent [--review]
  Mark done or submit for review (--review → IN_REVIEW)

kanban task view <id>
  View task detail, notes, and history

kanban task search [--status] [--role] [--agent] [--project]
  Filter task list

kanban task approve <id> --agent
  Move IN_REVIEW to DONE

kanban task reject <id> --agent --reason
  Return IN_REVIEW to TODO with reason

kanban task stats [--status] [--role]
  Aggregate task statistics
```

### Batch Operations

```
kanban task batch claim --ids --agent
  Claim multiple tasks atomically

kanban task batch complete --ids --agent [--review]
  Complete multiple tasks in one transaction

kanban task batch set-priority --ids --priority
  Set priority for multiple tasks

kanban task batch set-project --ids --project
  Set project label for multiple tasks
```

### Plan Management

```
kanban plan lint
  Check for structural problems: cycles, missing deps, unknown refs
```

### Other

```
kanban events [--task TASK-1]
  View event log, optionally filtered by task

kanban skill list
  List embedded skill definitions

kanban skill view <name>
  View an embedded skill definition

kanban status
  Show board status and progress

kanban prune
  Prune old events, history, and notes

kanban version
  Print version
```

---

## Skill Definitions (Protocol)

### manager: dispatch-task

Create a new task in the kanban board. Tasks start as TODO and are picked up by workers via claim-next.

```
kanban task dispatch \
  --title "Brief task description" \
  --role worker \
  --priority 50
```

Flags: --title (req), --role (req), --priority (no, default 100), --depends-on (no), --project (no)

Exit codes: 0 = task created, JSON on stdout. 2 = error, JSON on stderr.

### manager: create-plan

3-pass extraction from a plan file. Extract surface tasks, implied work, then integration sequencing. Writes proposal for user approval. Does NOT dispatch.

Pass 1 (Surface Scan): Extract every explicitly described deliverable. Be exhaustive — every file, component, endpoint, config value, page gets a task. Do not collapse related items.

Pass 2 (Implied Work): For every surface task, extract work the plan does not explicitly name but implementation requires. UI: loading/empty/error states, hover/focus/disabled, keyboard nav, screen readers, responsive layout. API: error responses, input validation, auth, rate limiting. Data: migrations, validation schemas, shared types.

Pass 3 (Integration + Sequencing): Dependency graph, phases, cross-cutting concerns, risk, gaps.

Output: `.kanban/tasks-proposal.md`. Stop after writing. Do NOT dispatch.

### manager: approve-plan

Read `.kanban/tasks-proposal.md`, find checked `[x]` items, dispatch each to the board.

```
kanban task dispatch --title "Implement user login" --priority 10 --role worker
```

Proposal format: numbered items with `[x]` or `[ ]`, (pN) priority, role: name markers. `--all` flag dispatches regardless of checkboxes.

### manager: review-backlog

List all tasks. Apply optional filters:

```
kanban task search                 # all tasks
kanban task search --status BLOCKED
kanban task search --role worker --status TODO
kanban task search --agent alice
```

Flags: --status (TODO/IN_PROGRESS/BLOCKED/IN_REVIEW/DONE), --role, --agent, --limit

### manager: view-task

Get full task details including all notes and history events.

```
kanban task view TASK-101
```

Returns: task object with id, title, status, role_boundary, priority, assigned_agent, lease_until, plus notes array and history array.

### manager: setup-hooks

Set up event hooks in `.kanban/hooks/`. Hook must be executable. Receives JSON on stdin. 30s timeout. `.d/` dir for concurrent hooks.

Events: task.created, task.claimed, task.progress, task.completed, task.submitted_for_review, task.blocked, review.approved, review.rejected, task.priority_updated, task.project_updated.

### worker: claim-next-task

Claim the highest-priority unclaimed task for your role. Also reclaims tasks with expired leases (15 min without heartbeat). Leases are lazy-reclaimed.

```
kanban task claim-next --agent my-agent --role worker
```

Only tasks matching role_boundary. Selects lowest priority, then oldest created_at. Claim is atomic.

Batch: `--count 3` returns JSON array of up to 3 tasks.

### worker: claim-task

Claim a specific task by ID. Task must be TODO (or IN_PROGRESS with expired lease). Unmet dependencies block the claim.

```
kanban task claim TASK-12 --agent worker-1
```

### worker: log-progress

Report progress on current task. Appends a note and renews the 15-minute lease.

```
kanban task log-progress TASK-101 --agent my-agent --note "Found root cause" --type PROGRESS
```

Note types: PROGRESS, ERROR, DECISION.

### worker: block-task

Mark current task as BLOCKED. Clears lease. Task held in BLOCKED state for manager review.

```
kanban task block TASK-101 --agent my-agent --reason "Waiting for upstream API"
```

### worker: complete-task

Mark current task as DONE. Use --review to submit for approval instead.

```
kanban task complete TASK-101 --agent my-agent          # → DONE
kanban task complete TASK-101 --agent my-agent --review  # → IN_REVIEW
```

For long-running tasks (>15 min): `kanban task extend-lease TASK-101 --agent my-agent --minutes 30`

### reviewer: claim-review

Claim the next unclaimed reviewer task (TODO tasks tagged role_boundary: reviewer). For IN_REVIEW work, use approve/reject directly.

```
kanban task claim-next --agent reviewer-agent --role reviewer
```

### reviewer: approve-task

Approve a task in IN_REVIEW state, marking it DONE.

```
kanban task approve TASK-101 --agent reviewer-agent
```

### reviewer: reject-task

Reject a task in IN_REVIEW state, sending it back to TODO for rework.

```
kanban task reject TASK-101 --agent reviewer-agent --reason "Missing test coverage"
```

---

## Role Workflows

### Manager

1. `kanban plan create plan.md` — 3-pass extraction, writes proposal
2. `kanban plan approve` — dispatch checked tasks to board
3. `kanban task dispatch` — create individual tasks
4. `kanban task search` / `kanban task view` — review backlog
5. `setup-hooks` — wire up Slack/Discord/metrics

### Worker

1. `kanban task claim-next --agent alice --role worker` — grab work
2. `kanban task log-progress TASK-1 --agent alice --note "..."` — heartbeat
3. `kanban task complete TASK-1 --agent alice` or `--review`
4. `kanban task block TASK-1 --agent alice --reason "..."` — when stuck

### Reviewer

1. `kanban task claim-next --agent bob --role reviewer` — find work
2. `kanban task approve TASK-5 --agent bob` — approve
3. `kanban task reject TASK-5 --agent bob --reason "..."` — reject
